sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id Int64, name Nullable(String), salary Nullable(Float64), categories Array(String)) ENGINE = Memory;

INSERT INTO test_data VALUES (1, 'Alice', 70000.5, ['manager', 'team lead']), (2, 'Bob', 50000.0, ['developer']), (3, NULL, 60000.0, ['developer', NULL]), (4, 'Eve', NULL, ['analyst']), (5, 'Charlie', 80000.12, ['manager']), (6, 'David', NULL, []);
SELECT argMin(name, salary) FROM test_data WHERE salary IS NOT NULL;
SELECT argMin(name, salary + 1000) FROM (SELECT name, salary FROM test_data WHERE salary IS NOT NULL) AS t;
SELECT argMin(name, salary) FROM test_data GROUP BY arrayElement(categories, 1) HAVING argMin(salary, salary) > 60000;
SELECT argMin(tuple(name, categories), salary) FROM test_data WHERE arrayJoin(categories) = 'developer';
SELECT argMin(name, arrayElement(categories, 2)) FROM test_data GROUP BY salary HAVING count() > 1;
SELECT argMin(arrayElement(categories, 1), salary + 100) AS minName, min(salary) FROM test_data WHERE salary IS NOT NULL GROUP BY categories;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE filter_data AS SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT argMin(name, (salary, id)) FROM filter_data GROUP BY categories;
SELECT argMin(name, min(salary)) FROM test_data WHERE name IS NULL OR salary IS NULL;
SELECT argMin(name, salary) FROM test_data WHERE name IS NOT NULL AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_data t2 WHERE t2.salary < test_data.salary);
